realization_fidelity.flattened_to: The implementation is a Riemann-sum sampling game (click curve to place sample points, accumulate fuel tokens, see estimated vs. true distance) rather than the specified predict-then-measure area-betting design (type/dial a numerical prediction, lock it in, watch liquid fill reveal true area).
violations: manipulated_variable: Student manipulates sample point placement (clicking on curve) and bound handles via drag, NOT a numerical prediction typed/dialed before revelation
primary_visual_feedback: Fill animation is driven by accumulated samples and Riemann sum, not by a locked prediction token competing against a live counter—the feedback loop is inverted

goal_structure: Missing the commit-a-prediction step entirely; instead student spends fuel tokens to click-sample, which is free exploration constrained by MAX_FUEL_TOKENS, not a predict-commit-then-reveal cycle

causal_chain_exposed: Code computes Riemann sum from sample points, but the design asks for 'running area counter shows rate of accumulation; fill speed visually correlates to curve height'—this pedagogical exposure of integration-as-accumulation is not present
trace_strategy: No ghost tokens or error bars tracking prediction history across trials; only current sample state is shown

aha_moment: The insight about narrow-tall vs. wide-short sections and concavity patterns is never elicited; the game structure (sampling + Riemann approximation) does not naturally lead students to that realization

polish.worst_offense: The teal compass/asterisk icon inside the circular badge on the curve violates the no-emoji/clip-art rule and adds decorative noise that doesn't convey mathematical meaning.
fixes: Remove the compass icon from the badge at (4s, ~20mph); replace with a simple dot or no badge at all if the point needs marking—the curve itself is the salient visual.
The label 'Samples: 0/8' at bottom right is too small (appears <11px) and low-contrast gray; increase to 12px, use ink-gray (#334155), and anchor it visually near the timeline or step indicator.

Add a faint hairline or tick mark at the time axis where each of the 8 sample points *could* go, or highlight the current sample slot interactively, to make the 'click to sample' affordance more discoverable and reduce cognitive load on first interaction.

issues: Frame 3 (areaIntuition_boundA=3.5): The circled interaction marker appears disconnected or floating above the curve rather than anchored to a specific point on the region boundary
Frames 2-4 show the curved region shape itself changing in height/profile rather than only the prediction bounds or fill state changing, suggesting the wrong variable is being manipulated
The prediction token and liquid-fill animation mechanic are not visibly present or changing across any frame; the intended 'commit-then-measure' interaction feedback loop is not demonstrated

Frame consistency: The region's geometric bounds and curve profile shift across frames, but no visual history of 'ghost prediction tokens' or error visualization accumulates as the trace_strategy intends

Frame 3 (areaIntuition_boundB=4.5): The prediction token and measurement region appear misaligned; the circular marker on the curve does not sit on the curve line itself, breaking the geometric constraint that the bound indicator must remain attached to the curve.

Frame 4 (areaIntuition_boundB=6.2): The shaded region under the curve narrows significantly but the curve itself does not visually support this narrowing—the x-axis bounds should expand with increasing boundB, but the curve's horizontal extent appears compressed relative to earlier frames.

Frames 3–5: The liquid fill visualization and running counter are not visible in any frame. The primary visual feedback mechanism (animated fill with live counter) described in the design is absent across the entire filmstrip, suggesting the interactive reveal state is not being triggered or displayed.

Frame 5 (areaIntuition_boundB=8.0): The prediction token position shifts far to the right, but there is no visual explanation for this shift; it should remain at a fixed location on screen while the measured region changes, not move with the bound itself.

visual_check_failures.expected: shaded region visible between bounds, no fill animation running, area value hidden or showing placeholder
visual_check_failures.observed: The two screenshots appear identical with no visible changes in shaded regions, fill animations, or area value displays.

results.description: On load, the shaded region is visible but area is not revealed - posing the challenge of estimating area under a curve
results.visual_check: shaded region visible between bounds, no fill animation running, area value hidden or showing placeholder

visual_verdict.observed: The two screenshots appear identical with no visible changes in shaded regions, fill animations, or area value displays.

results.description: The left bound (a) can be dragged along the x-axis
results.visual_check: left bound handle moves to x=2, shaded region shrinks from left

visual_verdict.observed: The left bound handle (teal circle) moved from x=0 to x=2, and the shaded region under the speed curve on the left side was removed.

results.description: The right bound (b) can be dragged along the x-axis
results.visual_check: right bound handle moves to x=4, shaded region shrinks from right

visual_verdict.observed: The right purple bound handle (b) moved left from x=8 to x=4, and the shaded region under the curve shrunk from the right side accordingly.

results.description: Dragging bounds causes the shaded region and area value to update in real-time
results.visual_check: shaded region expands to cover more area under curve, area readout increases

visual_verdict.observed: The purple handle (b) moved from position 4 seconds to position 8 seconds, expanding the shaded region under the curve to cover the entire visible speed curve area.

results.description: Clicking on the timeline to sample speed costs one fuel token
results.visual_check: a sample marker appears at the clicked x-position, fuel token counter decreases by 1

visual_verdict.observed: A sample marker appeared on the timeline and the fuel token counter decreased from 0/8 to 1/8.
